rationale: Ground truth has no substantive concerns (bot notice + plain 'lgtm'), so both APPROVE verdicts trivially match and recall is vacuously full for each. Precision favors Y: every line number Y cites (754, 1017, 1157, 1248, 1286, 1329 in api_server.py; 2533/2546/2615 in tests) matches the diff exactly when hand-verified against the provided hunks, and Y is explicit about checking the PR-time tree. X's specific line citations (1024, 2638/2651/2720) don't match the diff's line numbers at all — a consistent ~105-line offset suggests X grepped a later, evolved 'post-merge' checkout rather than the PR-time diff, making its claims harder to trust as grounded in this PR. Y's checklist-style validated/sweep items are also more concretely anchored (exact site-by-site line refs) than X's categorical PASS table, giving Y the actionability edge despite no changes being requested.
